sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.112 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Is it true that the orders of all elements of a finite group$G$are powers of primes if, for every divisor$d$($d>1$) of$|G|$and for every subgroup$H$of$G$of order coprime to$d$, the order$|H|$divides the number of elements of$G$of order$d$? The converse is true (W.\,J.\,Shi, Math. Forum (Vladikavkaz), { 6} (2012), 152--154). { W.\,J.\,Shi} Yes, it is true (A.\,A.\,Buturlakin, R.\,Shen, W.\,Shi, Sib...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
